meta.note: 口语题库(2026 版铺量)。Part 1 12 话题 · Part 2 15 cue card · Part 3 15 深入主题(与 P2 一一对应或按 2026 热题独立)。每题 book_ref 字段留空,供后续标注书籍/来源(如《雅思口语王陆真题》某章),支持从用户上传书籍 PDF 用 Qwen-VL 批量填充。

questions: What do you do? Do you work or study?
Why did you choose that subject / job?
What do you enjoy most about it?
What are your future plans in this field?
Where is your hometown?
Do you like your hometown? Why?
How has your hometown changed in recent years?
Would you recommend visitors to go there?
What do you usually do in your free time?
When did you start this hobby?
Do you prefer doing it alone or with others?
Do you think hobbies are important for people? Why?
What kind of food do you like?
Do you cook at home? Why or why not?
Is fast food popular in your country?
Has your diet changed over the last few years?
What's the weather like in your city?
Which season do you enjoy most?
Does the weather affect your mood or plans?
Has the climate changed since you were a child?
Do you like travelling? Why?
Where was the last place you visited?
Do you prefer travelling alone or with others?
Would you rather travel by train, plane or car? Why?
How often do you use your smartphone?
Which app do you use the most?
Do you think people rely too much on technology?
Has AI changed your daily life this year?
Do you live with your family?
Who are you closest to in your family?
Do families in your country spend enough time together?
How has family life changed compared with your parents' generation?
Do you play any sport or exercise regularly?
Did you enjoy sports at school?
Do you prefer team sports or individual ones?
Are sports popular among young people in your country?
What kind of music do you enjoy?
When do you usually listen to music?
Have you ever learned a musical instrument?
Is music important in your culture?
Do you read for pleasure?
What kind of books do you prefer?
Do you read paper books or e-books more?
Do you think reading habits have changed since smartphones became common?
Do you usually get enough sleep?
What do you do to stay healthy?
Has your diet changed in the last few years?
Do people in your country worry about their health enough?
What do you do when it's cold outside?
Which season is your favourite?
What do you do when it's cold outside?
Which season is your favourite?

You are going to hear questions that are typical of Part 1 of the exam. Listen to each question and give your answers using some of the vocabulary you have learnt so far in this unit. Record your answers.
Now listen to the sample answers. (See also Track 31, page 108.)
How often do you use the Internet for the following activities?
What are the dangers of using the Internet?
Have you experienced any of these dangers?
How do your habits, views and experiences differ from others?

You are going to hear Part 1 questions that are typical of the exam. Listen to each question and give your answer. Record yourself. Remember, do not give answers that are too short: saying just one word or one short sentence is not enough.
Would you rather live in a city or in the country?
What tense is used to describe how things were in the past?
What tenses are used to describe the current situation?
What is your nearest World Heritage Site?
Have you visited it?
Do you enjoy going on holiday with your parents?
Can you afford to go on holiday this year?
Were you skiing when you fell?
Are you sure?
Do you enjoy going on holiday with your parents?
Can you afford to go on holiday this year?
Were you skiing when you fell?
Are you sure?
What are the best ways to keep fit?
Do you think most people consider exercise a chore or do they find it fun?
What is the most popular form of exercise in your country?
Do you work or are you a student?
Why did you choose that course or job?
What is the most difficult thing about your studies or job?
Do you think science subjects are more useful than arts subjects?
Are students mature enough to choose what to study themselves or should their parents decide for them?
In your country, does having a university education help you into a better career?
Do you think older or younger workers are more motivated?
How can managers increase motivation among their workforce?
Are people more motivated if their job involves helping others?
Tell me about your country's climate.
What's your favourite kind of weather?
Does the weather influence your mood?
Is it worse to feel too hot or too cold?
Are people in your country concerned about protecting the environment?
Does the responsibility for protecting the environment lie with governments or with individuals?
Does it help to educate young people on being green?
What effects does pollution have on the environment?
Do you think we pollute more now than we did 50 years ago?
What do you think will happen if we do not reduce current levels of pollution?
What's your mother tongue?
What other languages do you speak?
What do you think is the best way to keep in touch with friends?
Do people keep in touch differently now compared to fifty years ago?
What do you think of online shopping?
What do people in your country think of new technology?
What are your hobbies?
What is your favourite musical instrument?
Do you prefer action films or comedies? Why?
Do you think it is important to read novels and poetry? Why?
How long have you lived here?
Have you ever eaten Italian food?
What has happened in the news today?
What have you done so far today?
How long have you been doing your favourite hobby?
How many times have you engaged in your hobby this week, or this year?
Have you had less time for your hobby since you started studying for IELTS?
Do you think men and women tend to have different types of hobbies?
Why do some people get obsessed with their hobby?
Do you think hobbies that keep you fit are better than hobbies that you can do sitting down?
Do you think it can be a disadvantage to have too much free time?
Should people feel a duty to do something constructive in their free time?
Do people have more free time now than in the past?
What was your favourite toy when you were a child?
Is it important for children to have fun? Why?
Tell me about your hometown.
In what ways has your town or city changed since you were a child?
How could your town or city be improved?
Are there any traffic problems where you live?
Is it only children who experience homesickness?
Do you think it's better for children to grow up in the city or the country?
Why do some people retire to the countryside?
Are people from your country patriotic?
Why do people often feel proud of where they come from?
Does intense patriotism have any disadvantages?
What is the most important festival in your country?
Do you think this festival will still be as important in the future?
Tell me how weddings are celebrated in your country.
What are some forms of traditional dancing in your country?
Are historical sites in your country popular with visitors?
Is it more important to preserve historical sites or make way for the developments of the future?
What do you think will happen to your country's historical sites in the future?
Would you feel nervous about going on holiday alone?
Do you enjoy travelling with your family?
Are you scared of flying?
Have you been away this year?
Do you like to try and learn the language of the country you're travelling to?
Are there any differences between what young people like to do on holiday and what older people like to do?
Did you have a good time on your last holiday?
Do you mind roughing it?
If you won £1,000,000, would you spend it on a round-the-world trip?
What is your favourite animal?
Why do you think some people like keeping pets?
Are there any animals you are scared of?
Are zoos popular in your country?
Let's move on to talk about food. Do you think men or women make the best cooks?
Is it important to teach children to cook from a young age?
What is a typical dish from your country or region?
Do people in your country or region eat traditional food or international food?
What is your favourite animal?
Why do you think some people like keeping pets?
Are there any animals you are scared of?
Are zoos popular in your country?
Which sports do you most enjoy watching or playing, and which do you least enjoy? Why?
Do you work or are you a student?
Why did you choose that course or job?
What is the most difficult thing about your studies or job?
Tell me something about your family.
What do you like doing most with your family?
Who are you close to in your family?
In what way is your family important to you?
What do you do to keep fit?
Are you good at sport?
What sports do you play?
What is the most popular sport in your country?

part2.topic: A place you would like to visit
part2.cue:
You should say:
- where it is
- how you first heard about it
- what you would do there
- and explain why you would like to visit this place.

part2.topic: A useful skill you learned
part2.cue:
You should say:
- what the skill is
- when and how you learned it
- who taught you (if anyone)
- and explain why this skill is useful for you.

part2.topic: An important decision you made
part2.cue:
You should say:
- what the decision was
- when you made it
- why you made this decision
- and explain how this decision has affected your life.

part2.topic: An old thing your family has kept for a long time
part2.cue:
You should say:
- what it is
- who it belongs to
- how long your family has had it
- and explain why it is important to your family.

part2.topic: A memorable meal you had
part2.cue:
You should say:
- when and where you had it
- who you had it with
- what you ate
- and explain why the meal was memorable.

part2.topic: A creative person you admire
part2.cue:
You should say:
- who the person is
- how you know about them
- what they have created
- and explain why you think they are creative.

part2.topic: A time a stranger helped you
part2.cue:
You should say:
- when and where it happened
- who the stranger was
- what they did
- and explain how you felt afterwards.

part2.topic: A new hobby you would like to try
part2.cue:
You should say:
- what the hobby is
- when you first heard about it
- what you would need to start
- and explain why you would like to try it.

part2.topic: A teacher who influenced you when you were a child
part2.cue:
You should say:
- who the teacher was
- what subject they taught
- what they were like
- and explain how they influenced you.

part2.topic: Something you enjoy doing on a rainy day
part2.cue:
You should say:
- what the activity is
- when you started doing it on rainy days
- who you usually do it with
- and explain why you enjoy it on rainy days.

part2.topic: A gift you gave to someone
part2.cue:
You should say:
- what the gift was
- who you gave it to
- why you chose that gift
- and explain how the person reacted.

part2.topic: A small business you would like to start
part2.cue:
You should say:
- what the business would be
- where you would set it up
- who your customers would be
- and explain why you think it would succeed.

part2.topic: A traditional festival in your country
part2.cue:
You should say:
- what the festival is
- when it is celebrated
- what people usually do
- and explain why it is important to your culture.

part2.topic: A historical building you have visited
part2.cue:
You should say:
- what and where the building is
- when you visited it
- what you saw or learned there
- and explain why the building is historically important.

part2.topic: An online course you took (or would like to take)
part2.cue:
You should say:
- what the course is (or would be) about
- where you found it
- how long it lasted (or will last)
- and explain what you gained (or hope to gain) from it.

part2.topic: A language other than English that you would like to learn
part2.cue:
You should say:
- what the language is
- where it is spoken
- what you think would be difficult and what would be easy about learning the language
- and explain why you would like to learn it.

part2.topic: A piece of electronic equipment
part2.cue:
You should say:
- what the piece of equipment is
- when and where you got it
- what you use it for
- and explain why you find it useful.

part2.topic: A hobby you enjoy
part2.cue:
Describe a hobby you enjoy.
You should say:
- how long you have been doing it
- how often you do it
- what benefits you get from it
and explain why you enjoy it.

part2.topic: A happy childhood memory
part2.cue:
You should say:
- when and where the incident you remember happened
- who you were with
- what happened exactly
- and explain why it is a happy memory.

part2.topic: A place that you enjoy visiting
part2.cue:
You should say:
- where the place is
- what you can see and do there
- how it has changed since you first visited it
- and explain why you enjoy visiting this place.

part2.topic: A festival that you enjoy
part2.cue:
You should say:
- when the festival occurs
- where it occurs
- what happens during it
- and explain why you enjoy the festival.

part2.topic: A memorable holiday
part2.cue:
You should say:
- when you went on this holiday
- where you went
- what you did
- and explain why it is memorable.

part2.topic: The most exciting tennis match I've ever seen
part2.cue:
You should say:
- when and where it was
- who was involved
- what happened during the match
- and explain why it was so exciting

part2.topic: Childhood Memory
part2.cue:
You should say:
- What the memory is
- When it happened
- Who was involved
- And explain why it is your most vivid childhood memory

part2.topic: Hometown
part2.cue:
Tell me about your hometown.
In what ways has your town or city changed since you were a child?
How could your town or city be improved?
Are there any traffic problems where you live?

part2.topic: A memorable holiday
part2.cue:
You should say:
- when and where you went
- who you went with
- what you did
- and explain why it was memorable

part2.topic: A personal achievement you are proud of
part2.cue:
You should say:
- what the achievement was
- when and where it happened
- why it was important to you
- and explain why you are proud of it.

part2.topic: An exciting competition or sporting event
part2.cue:
You should say:
- what the competition or sporting event was
- when and where it took place
- who won
- and explain why it was exciting.

part2.topic: Favourite Season
part2.cue:
You should say:
- what the season is and when it occurs
- what the weather is like during this season
- what your typical activities are during this season
- and explain why it is your favourite season.

part2.topic: A personal achievement you are proud of
part2.cue:
You should say:
- what you achieved
- when you achieved it
- what was difficult about achieving it
- and explain why you are proud of this achievement.

questions: Why do young people and older people travel for different reasons?
How has the way people travel changed in the last 30 years?
Do you think travelling is important for personal development? Why?
Some people say international tourism does more harm than good — do you agree?
Which skills do you think today's children should learn at school?
Is it easier to learn new skills when you are young? Why?
How has the internet changed the way people learn new skills?
Do you think traditional skills like handwriting or cooking are still important today?
Do you think young people are good at making important decisions?
Should parents make big decisions for their children? Why or why not?
How do people usually deal with regret after a bad decision?
Is it better to make decisions quickly or take a lot of time?
Why do some families keep old things for generations?
Do you think museums are important? Why?
Is it worth spending money to preserve old buildings and cities?
How does modern life affect people's connection with traditions?
How do eating habits differ between your country and Western countries?
Do you think fast food is changing traditional cuisine?
Is it important for families to eat meals together? Why?
How is food connected with culture in your country?
What makes a person creative?
Can creativity be taught, or is it something you're born with?
How has AI changed creative work like art, music or writing?
Do you think AI-generated content should be labelled as such? Why?
Are people in your country generally willing to help strangers?
Why do some people avoid helping strangers?
How can we encourage more kindness in cities?
Do you think social media makes people more or less connected in real life?
Why is it important for adults to have hobbies?
How have hobbies changed compared with 20 years ago?
Do children today have enough time for hobbies? Why?
What are the benefits of picking up a new hobby as an adult?
What makes a good teacher?
Should teachers be paid more? Why?
How is online learning different from classroom learning?
Do parents or teachers have a bigger influence on children? Why?
How does weather affect people's daily lives?
Do you think climate change is affecting weather patterns where you live?
Young people around the world talk about "climate anxiety" — what do you think of that?
What can ordinary people do to slow down climate change?
In your culture, when do people give gifts?
Do you think it's the thought that counts, or the price of the gift?
How has online shopping changed the way people choose gifts?
Are handmade gifts still valued today? Why?

part3.topic: Work, small businesses & hybrid work (2026 hot)
questions: Why do some people prefer running a small business to working for a big company?
What difficulties do young entrepreneurs face today?
Has "hybrid work" (mixing office + home) changed how people think about jobs?
Do you think most people will change careers several times in their life?
Why is it important to keep traditional festivals alive?
How do young people in your country view traditional culture?
Do you think globalisation is making cultures more similar? Is that a problem?
How can traditional culture be presented to attract younger generations?
Should governments spend a lot of money on protecting old buildings?
Why do people enjoy visiting historical sites?
How can technology help preserve history?
Do you think studying history is useful for young people today? Why?
How has online learning changed education in the past few years?
Are online courses as effective as traditional ones? Why?
Should learning platforms be allowed to collect student data? Why or why not?
How can people protect their digital privacy while studying online?

part3.topic: Pronunciation of consonants and consonant clusters

questions: You are going to hear questions that are typical of Part 3 of the exam. Listen to each question and give your answer. Put into practice all you have learnt in this unit.
Now listen to the sample answers.
You are going to hear questions that are typical of Part 3 of the exam. Listen to each question and give your answers. Record your answers.

Listen to your recording, assessing your use of the vocabulary, grammar and pronunciation you have studied in this unit. Re-record your answers, trying to improve your performance in the areas where you were weaker.
Now listen to the sample answers.

part3.topic: General discussion on pronunciation and contractions
questions: You are going to hear questions that are typical of Part 3 of the exam. Answer them and record your answers.
Ask a friend or teacher to listen carefully to your recording. Are there any words you pronounced incorrectly?
Practise saying the words with accurate pronunciation.
Keep a list of words you have mispronounced and test yourself frequently.
You are going to hear some questions that are typical of Part 3 of the exam. Answer the questions, putting the language you have learnt in this unit into practice.
Do you think people often idealise their childhoods?
How does a person's childhood influence what kind of adult they become?
When does a child become an adult in your view?
Do you agree with the saying 'children should be seen and not heard'?
Is it good for children to be exposed to frightening and sad experiences or should they be protected from these as far as possible?
Are children in your country generally well brought up?
Do you think it's true that travel broadens the mind?
Do young people and older people benefit differently from travelling?
How can you make sure you get the most from your travels?
What are the positive impacts of tourism?
What about the negative impacts of tourism?
How has tourism impacted tourist sites in your country?

part3.topic: Achievements and motivation in education and workplace
questions: Do you think that in your country academic success is more valued than other kinds of achievement, such as achievements in sport?
Why do you think that is?
In your opinion, is it recognition and prizes that motivate students to succeed, or is it a personal sense of achievement?
What do you think makes some students more successful than others?
Do you agree that a successful person is someone who earns a lot of money?
And what about how things in the workplace have changed? Do you think people in your country take the same pride in their work as they used to?
My answer may surprise you but, in my view, the most interesting sport is figure skating. I'm always on the edge of my seat, willing the skater not to fall. It's so nail-biting.

If you ask me, the best sport for keeping fit is without doubt swimming. It is well known that swimming uses all the major muscle groups. Not only that, it's cardiovascular exercise, which strengthens the heart and lungs.

I would argue that the most dangerous sport is sky diving, for obvious reasons. I know someone whose parachute failed to open properly, and she considered herself extremely lucky to just break both her legs.
I would say the most dangerous sports are those involving fast and powerful vehicles, like motor racing. There are very often crashes, resulting in not only injuries but on occasion fatalities too.
Do you work or are you a student?
Why did you choose that course or job?
What is the most difficult thing about your studies or job?
What do you think about the article?
Do you find the report worrying?
What is your country doing to reduce its footprint?
What are the environmental consequences of the lifestyles that the people in your country lead?
Do some research, make some notes, and then discuss the topic with someone. How do your views differ?
In what ways have families changed in the last hundred years?
Should we rely heavily on our families or is it better to try to be independent?
The elderly are sometimes considered the wisest members of society. What do you think we can learn from them?
What do you expect from a good friend?
Are friends as important to you as family?
Do you think friendships change as we get older? How?

part3.topic: Language learning and English as a global language
questions: Is it considered important in your country to learn foreign languages?
What, in your opinion, is the best way to learn a language?
Why are some people seemingly better at learning languages than others?
How do people in your country feel about English being the world language?
Do you think the culture of English-speaking countries, as well as the English language, dominate the world?
Why do you think people feel it is important to continue speaking their local languages?
